review_execution.structured_output_effort_field: Moderate. Full analysis of the unified diff across 8 files plus the S19 contract/fixture snapshots, cross-checking policy/module/validator/fixture/test consistency and scope-boundary flags. No local file-read tools were available, so verification leaned on the provided diff and the pre-review command evidence (node --check, node --test 413/413, contract validator).

findings.title: Policy assert uses inclusion-only checks for several arrays (no length/exact-match guard)
findings.description: assertControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingUnitTestHappyPathPolicy enforces exact length only for requiredInputFields and testedSubphaseIds. For testedExports, goldenAssertions, fixtureIntegrityRules, bindingContextIds, and acceptanceGateIds it uses requireArrayIncludesAll (inclusion only), so extra/appended entries in those arrays would not be caught by the policy assertion itself. Drift is still caught by the contract validator's JSON.stringify equality and the test-file deepEquals, and this mirrors the established pattern in prior M05 subphases, so risk is low.
findings.recommendation: Defer. Optionally add length or exact-equality guards for the remaining arrays in a future consistency pass to make the policy assertion self-sufficient.

findings.title: Golden chain test asserts S02-S18 refs explicitly but not the S01 entrypoint contract ref
findings.description: The 'validates S01 through S18 golden chain' test explicitly asserts the per-subphase refs for S02 through S18 and entrypoint_id, but does not explicitly assert service_entrypoint_contract_ref === 'RP00.P02.M05.S01'. S01 coverage is transitive via assertControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRetryBehaviorResult(result) and the fixture deepEqual in the adjacent fixture test, so the chain is still fully verified.
findings.recommendation: Defer. Optionally add an explicit assert.equal(result.service_entrypoint_contract_ref, 'RP00.P02.M05.S01') so the test name's S01 claim is asserted directly.

findings.title: Live-mirror check depends on JSON.stringify key-order equality
findings.description: The validator compares the fixture golden_result to the live precheckControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRetryBehavior output via JSON.stringify equality, which is sensitive to key ordering. It passes today (the fixture was authored to match emission order), but it is a brittle coupling if the precheck object's key order ever changes. This is a pre-existing pattern reused across all M05 subphases, not introduced or worsened by S19.
findings.recommendation: Defer. If revisited project-wide, switch to an order-insensitive deep-equality comparison for the golden_result mirror.

security_notes: Fixture is synthetic-only: tenant.synthetic.lawfirm, user.synthetic.permission-audit, matter.synthetic.control-plane; fixture_integrity_rules enforce synthetic_only / no_real_client_data / no_real_matter_data / no_credentials_or_secrets.
No secrets, credentials, or real client/matter data present in the diff.
No runtime I/O introduced: only readFile of the local synthetic fixture inside tests; no DB/storage/audit/retry writes, no network, no eval/dynamic code, no shell — no injection surface.
Product boundary intact: diff is confined to packages/control-plane, the control-plane contract, the validator script, and the README; no product runtime, HRX, or G30 code touched.
All write/execution capabilities are explicitly denied at the data level (every *_permitted flag false), reinforcing the metadata-only boundary.
test_notes: Pre-review evidence: node --test packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js PASS 413/413; node --check PASS on all five touched JS files; git diff --check PASS.
Four new S19 tests: policy binding, S01-S18 golden chain, fixture golden-proof + synthetic integrity, and module no-write flags.

Golden proof is legitimate for a happy-path deliverable: proof_type golden_unit_test, failing_then_passing_or_golden=golden; deterministic result construction asserted against the synthetic fixture golden_result via deepEqual.

Contract validator adds an S19 coverage block: weighted boundary (terminal false, next S20), closeout flag, definition field/array checks, policy+module object checks, function-export checks, fixture checks, live precheck mirror, and README/test source-text presence; construction-inspection gate requires control_plane_permission_audit_binding_unit_test_happy_path_verified for the S19 closeout.

rp00:control-plane:validate currently fails only on the not-yet-created docs/closeout-packs/cp00-015/* evidence files (expected); that the run reports only those errors confirms prior-subphase fixture variables referenced in the new block (fixturePermissionAuditBindingRollbackBehavior / RetryBehavior) resolve correctly.
Minor: the 'S01 through S18' test does not explicitly assert the S01 entrypoint contract ref (covered transitively); see P3 finding.
raw_review.summary: CP00-015 (RP00.P02.M05.S19 'Unit test: happy path') is a clean, scope-compliant, test/metadata-only pack. It adds a frozen policy, a metadata module + getter, a pure validator, four tests, and a synthetic fixture, plus contract/script/README wiring. No P0/P1/P2 issues. S19 correctly does NOT claim RP00.P02.M05 completion and forbids all runtime/write/retry/audit/Hermes execution (all *_permitted flags false, all doesNot* flags true); M05 stays open and hands off to S20 (denied path) consistently across contract, policy, module, fixture, and weighted ledger. The S01-S18 golden chain is proven via deterministic result construction, full-object assertion, and a live-vs-fixture deepEqual mirror; the contract validator cross-checks definition, policy/module objects, fixture, source/README text, weighted boundary, and construction-inspection. Pre-review evidence (node --check PASS; node --test 413/413 PASS; contract validator passing except for the deliberately-uncreated pack files) corroborates internal consistency. Approve as production-ready once the standard CP00-015 evidence files (manifest, command-evidence, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are generated. Three P3 observations are noted for deferral.

adjudication_notes: One completed claude-opus-4-8 max read-only/no-tools review is recorded for CP00-015.
Claude returned approve with zero P0, zero P1, and zero P2 findings.
Three P3 findings are explicitly deferred because they are low-risk consistency hardening items and do not block production_ready under the pack policy.
The expected pre-evidence RP00 failure is resolved by creating this CP00-015 evidence and rerunning final validation.
